fragility_points: A 5%+ downside BTC move before Apr. 20 would erode the current cushion quickly.
Binance-specific dislocation at the resolution minute could matter more than broad spot.
key_assumptions: BTC remains roughly in the current mid-74k regime over the next five days.
No major downside catalyst hits before Apr. 20 noon ET.
Binance BTC/USDT does not materially diverge downward from broader BTC spot at settlement.
main_logical_chain: Start from the live market prior of 87.5% Yes.
Verify that current BTC price context is materially above 70k using external live references.
Audit the contract mechanics: Apr. 20, 12:00 ET, Binance BTC/USDT, final 1m candle Close, strictly greater than 70000.
Conclude the market is mostly pricing the setup correctly, with only a moderate discount needed for volatility and exact-minute settlement risk.
main_thesis: The market's 87.5% Yes price for BTC above 70000 on Apr. 20 looks broadly efficient given current mid-74k spot context, with remaining discount to certainty explained mainly by exact-minute Binance settlement risk and normal crypto volatility.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
source_quality_view: Primary source quality is strong for rules and baseline; secondary price verification is adequate but only moderately independent, so confidence is medium rather than maximal.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact Binance 1-minute close at 12:00 ET, so ordinary crypto volatility and venue-specific microstructure can still break an otherwise bullish thesis.
strongest_supports: Current BTC context around 74.2k-74.4k leaves roughly 6% cushion above the 70k strike.
The Polymarket strike ladder is internally coherent, with 74k near even odds and 70k much higher.
timing_relevance: Timing is central because the contract resolves on the Apr. 20, 2026 12:00 ET one-minute Binance BTC/USDT candle close, not a broader daily or cross-exchange price.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much of the high-80s price reflects true information aggregation versus in-the-money strike preference.
Whether exchange-specific settlement microstructure deserves a larger discount than applied here.
what_would_change_view: I would move lower if BTC loses the low-72k area, if a major downside catalyst emerges, or if Binance BTC/USDT shows meaningful weakness versus broader spot ahead of settlement.
